qa_pairs.question: How do I request a single full H100-80gb GPU for my compute job using Slurm?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one full H100-80gb GPU, you need to use one of the following Slurm options: `--gpus=h100:1` or `--gpus=h100_80gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm options are available for requesting multiple full H100-80gb GPUs per node?
qa_pairs.answer: For multiple H100-80gb GPUs per node, you can use the Slurm options: `--gpus-per-node=h100:2`, `--gpus-per-node=h100:3`, or `--gpus-per-node=h100:4`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I request multiple full H100 GPUs to be spread across different nodes for my job?
qa_pairs.answer: To request multiple full H100 GPUs spread anywhere, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100:n`, replacing 'n' with the number of GPUs you want.

qa_pairs.question: Are all GPU nodes on Nibi configured with Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology?
qa_pairs.answer: Approximately half of the GPU nodes are configured with Multi-Instance GPU (MIG technology).

qa_pairs.question: What are the three GPU instance sizes available for Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology?
qa_pairs.answer: The three GPU instance sizes available are H100-1g.10gb, H100-2g.20gb, and H100-3g.40gb.

qa_pairs.question: What are the characteristics of the H100-1g.10gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The H100-1g.10gb GPU instance provides 1/8th of the computing power with 10GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: Can you describe the H100-2g.20gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The H100-2g.20gb GPU instance offers 2/8th of the computing power with 20GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications for the H100-3g.40gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The H100-3g.40gb GPU instance provides 3/8th of the computing power with 40GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm option should I use to request one H100-1g.10gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-1g.10gb GPU instance, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100_1g.10gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a single H100-2g.20gb GPU instance for my compute job?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-2g.20gb GPU instance, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100_2g.20gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Slurm command for requesting one H100-3g.40gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The Slurm option for requesting one H100-3g.40gb GPU instance is `--gpus=h100_3g.40gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: Do Nibi compute nodes have internet access?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, all nodes on Nibi have Internet access, and no special firewall permission or proxying is necessary.

qa_pairs.question: Are user directories created by default on the `/project` space on Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: No, user directories are no longer created by default on `/project`.

qa_pairs.question: How can users create their own directories within their group's `/project` space?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can always create their own directories in the group's `/project` using `mkdir`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the soft quota limit for scratch space on Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: An 1 TB soft quota on scratch applies to each user.

qa_pairs.question: How long can a user exceed their scratch soft quota before it is enforced?
qa_pairs.answer: A user can exceed their scratch soft quota for up to 60 days.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after the 60-day grace period for the scratch soft quota expires?
qa_pairs.answer: After the grace period expires, the soft limit is enforced, meaning no further file creation or expansion is permitted until usage drops below the soft limit.

qa_pairs.question: How can one access the Nibi cluster through a web browser?
qa_pairs.answer: One can access the Nibi cluster simply through a web browser by using Open OnDemand (OOD).

qa_pairs.question: What features does Nibi's Open OnDemand (OOD) platform offer?
qa_pairs.answer: Nibi's Open OnDemand (OOD) is a web-based platform that provides a web interface to the login nodes and a remote desktop environment.

qa_pairs.question: What is the web address to log in to Nibi via Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: To log in to Nibi via Open OnDemand, go to `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca/` and sign in with MFA.

qa_pairs.question: What options are presented on the Open OnDemand interface after logging in?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in to Open OnDemand, you will see a user-friendly interface offering options to open a Bash shell terminal or launch a remote desktop session.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to run JupyterLab interactively on Nibi through Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can run JupyterLab interactively via the Nibi Open OnDemand portal.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to launch a pre-configured JupyterLab session from the Nibi Open OnDemand portal?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in to the Nibi Open OnDemand portal, click “Compute Node” from the top menu, select “Nibi JupyterLab,” complete the form with your requirement details, click “Launch,” and once the status changes to Running, click “Connect to Jupyter.”
